commit.message:
ci: split monolithic CI/CD pipeline into three workflows (#26)

Split the single Build/Test/Release workflow (ci.yml) into three
separate, focused workflows:

1. ci.yml (Build/Test) -- runs on PRs, pushes to main, and manual
   dispatch. Handles validation, linting, testing, security scanning,
   image builds, plugin builds, and multi-arch manifests. Images are
   pushed to GHCR only on push to main. The version job uses git
   describe instead of bump logic. The release job and all release-
   related inputs (bump, draft, prerelease, dry_run) are removed.

2. prepare-release.yml (Prepare Release) -- manual workflow_dispatch
   with a bump level input (patch/minor/major). Computes the next
   semver from the latest tag, creates a release/v{version} branch,
   inserts the version heading into CHANGELOG.md, and opens a PR
   to main.

3. release.yml (Release) -- triggers via workflow_run after Build/Test
   completes on main. Checks if the merge came from a release/v*
   branch; if so, extracts the version from CHANGELOG.md, creates
   and pushes the git tag, re-tags per-arch images with the release
   version using imagetools, re-renders Kubernetes manifests with
   proper image references, downloads plugin binaries from the CI
   run, generates and signs an SBOM, and creates a draft GitHub
   release.

This separation provides clearer responsibility boundaries: CI runs
on every code change, release preparation is an explicit human-
triggered step, and the actual release is fully automated after the
release PR merges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: production-grade dual-ML pipeline with full dashboard integration

Pipeline upgrades:
- ADC Corrector now fully functional with training pipeline
- Realistic ADC non-linearity simulation (INL + DNL errors)
- LSTM model upgraded with output projection layer and multi-layer support
- Dynamic threshold saved to JSON and loaded by agent (no hardcoded values)
- Data preprocessing uses anomaly labels instead of hardcoded row indices

Live agent rewrite:
- Removed hardcoded API key (now reads from .env)
- Both ML stages active with full metadata sent to dashboard
- Publishes rawValue, correctedValue, reconstructionError, threshold, pipeline status
- Structured logging, graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
- Centralized config.py for all hyperparameters and paths

Cleanup:
- Removed committed __pycache__, lightning_logs, checkpoints, data CSVs, plot PNGs
- Added .gitignore to prevent re-committing generated artifacts
- Removed unused dependencies (seaborn, torchvision), added missing ones (ably, dotenv)
- Added .env.example for onboarding
- Complete README with architecture, data flow, and setup guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(interp): add global epoch guard to prototype-chain IC

Cherry-picked from agent commit 408fb4bd.
Adds ProtoLoadIc.proto_global_epoch field and probe_epoch() method
that skips expensive proto_generation() call when the global epoch
is unchanged, reducing per-access overhead in prototype chain lookups.

commit.message:
chore: bump version to 0.0.57

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: orthographic camera for top-down view

- Swap to OrthographicCamera when entering top-down preset; swap back to
  PerspectiveCamera when leaving. Camera position is copied at swap so
  there is no visible jump.
- Animate ortho zoom (instead of FOV) during the transition; lerp is the
  same LERP_FACTOR as position so everything arrives together.
- Keep ortho frustum in sync with viewport resize.
- OrbitControls: use minZoom/maxZoom for top-down (scroll-to-zoom changes
  camera.zoom for ortho) and minDistance/maxDistance for perspective/iso.
- Fix WASD/arrow panning in top-down: camera directly above makes the
  forward-vector degenerate to zero; use fixed world cardinals instead
  (+Z forward, +X right).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(spec): document collection type-boundary restrictions (#886)

- §3.3.1: note that [T] slice annotations are rejected; only [T; N] fixed
  arrays are supported (MLIR has no slice lowering)
- §3.7.5 (Rc): add bullet that Rc<T> cannot appear as a collection element
  or key type in Vec, HashMap, or HashSet
- §3.10.3 (Vec): add element-restriction note — Rc<T> elements and element
  types that structurally contain fixed-size arrays are rejected
- §3.10.3 (HashMap): extend implementation boundary note to cover Rc<T>
  rejection in key and value positions
- §3.10.4 (HashSet): extend shipped-collections note to cover Rc<T> element
  rejection

All changes reflect current checker behaviour introduced in the type-boundary
hardening work; no speculative future support is described.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: migrate database from SQLite to MongoDB (#18)

## Summary

Migrates the backend from SQLite (SQLAlchemy) to MongoDB (Motor async),
fixes architecture and coding standards violations identified in a
project review against `REQUIREMENTS.md` and `copilot-instructions.md`.

## Changes

### 🔴 Database migration (MongoDB)
- Replace `SQLAlchemy`/`aiosqlite` with `motor==3.4.0` +
`pymongo==4.6.2` in `pyproject.toml`
- Rewrite `adapters/db/session.py`: async Motor client, reads
`MONGODB_URI` env var
- Rewrite `adapters/db/repository.py`: fully async `LinkRepository`
using Motor
- Delete `adapters/db/models.py` (SQLAlchemy ORM no longer needed)
- Update `docker-compose.yaml`: add `mongo:7` service, replace
`SQLITE_DB_PATH` with `MONGODB_URI`

### 🏗️ Clean Architecture fix
- Add `domain/ports.py` with `LinkRepositoryPort` Protocol — adapters
now implement a domain interface
- All API route handlers `await` async repository calls
- `lifespan()` creates MongoDB indexes instead of SQL tables

### 🟡 Standards compliance
- Remove `from __future__ import annotations` from 4 domain modules
(prohibited by coding standards)
- Fix `TOKEN_PEPPER` env var mismatch between `docker-compose.yaml` and
`deps.py`
- Add `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: lint (ruff + mypy), tests (pytest),
and frontend build on push/PR

### 🎨 Frontend — Svelte 5
- Upgrade `svelte` from `^4.2.18` to `^5.0.0` and
`@sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte` to `^4.0.0`
- Migrate `App.svelte` to Svelte 5 runes: `$state()`, `onclick`
(replaces `on:click`), typed error handling

### 🧪 Tests
- Rewrite `conftest.py` with `mongomock-motor` in-process MongoDB mock
- Rename + rewrite `test_repository_sqlite.py` →
`test_repository_mongo.py` (async)
- Update `test_id_token.py`: `generate_unique_link_id` is now `async`
- Update `test_api_crud.py`: use `override_db` fixture, align env var
name

## Test results
```
29 passed in 0.19s
```
Ruff and mypy both pass with zero errors.

## Out of scope (future work)
- Redis caching layer
- Rate limiting (429)
- Domain blocklist / abuse controls  
- `robots.txt` endpoint
- Extracting use-cases layer from `app.py`

---------

Co-authored-by: Jaroslaw Wencel <jaroslaw@wencel.it>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: bundle MCP server in VSIX instead of npx fallback

The extension was falling back to 'npx @jagilber-org/index-server' which
doesn't exist on npm. Now the build script bundles the server by default
and the extension checks <extensionPath>/server/dist/ before falling
back to npx.

- resolveServerPath: check bundled server in extension directory
- build-vsix.ps1: bundle server by default (-SkipServer to opt out)
- npx fallback now logs a warning (last resort only)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: cap notification queue to prevent unbounded growth

Add MAX_QUEUE_SIZE (100) and prune from front in push() and
push_persistent() when the queue exceeds the cap. Prevents memory
growth proportional to error count over long-running sessions.

fix: add drop guard for refresh in-flight flags

Introduce RefreshGuard that sends a fallback error message if the
spawned refresh task exits without sending a result (e.g., due to a
panic). Applied to both spawn_data_refresh and spawn_log_refresh.
Also exit follow mode when no active task is found on timeline
refresh, preventing stale follow state after build completion.

commit.message:
fix: atomic symlink replacement during self-update

Replace the check-then-remove-then-create pattern with atomic
create-temp-then-rename. This prevents a broken state if the symlink
creation fails after the old one is removed, which would leave the
user with no working binary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use TOML serializer for config to prevent injection

Replace format! string interpolation in Config::write_initial with
proper toml serialization via toml::to_string_pretty. This correctly
escapes special characters (quotes, newlines) in organization and
project names, preventing structurally broken config files.

commit.message:
fix: widen log scroll offset to u32 to support logs >65K lines

Change log_scroll_offset from u16 to u32 to prevent silent truncation
when log output exceeds 65,535 lines. The scroll_up, scroll_down, and
set_log_scroll_offset methods now operate on u32. The final value is
clamped to u16 only at the ratatui Paragraph::scroll boundary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix idempotency bug in transaction table name quoting

Previous regex used \btransaction\b which also matches inside
`transaction` (backtick is non-word char), producing ``transaction``
— invalid SQL — when the AI already quotes the table name.

New regex uses (FROM|JOIN|,)(\s+)`?transaction`? which consumes any
existing backticks before replacing, so the result is always a single
`transaction`. Safe whether the AI quotes or not.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix homepage CSS — replace hardcoded hashed stylesheet link with proper Astro import

The previous link href pointed to a build artifact hash that changes
on every deploy. Replace with a frontmatter CSS import so Astro
bundles and injects it correctly on every build.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(B-20): add past-turn isolation guards for A2UI surfaces

In multi-turn conversations, A2UI surfaces from previous turns are now
visually dimmed (opacity: 0.5) and non-interactive (pointer-events: none,
user-select: none). Only the latest turn's surface stays fully active.

Changes:
- A2UISurfaceWrapper: add `isActive` prop (default true); when false,
  apply opacity + pointer-events/user-select none styles
- ChatMessage: thread `isActive` prop down to A2UISurfaceWrapper
- MessageList: pass `isActive={index === messages.length - 1}` so only
  the last message's surface is interactive
- Playground Create tab: same `isActive` guard on the inline message loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(starrocks): grant SELECT on Iceberg catalog tables for readonly role

The readonly role had USAGE on ol_data_lake_iceberg (making the catalog
visible) but lacked the table-level SELECT grant within that catalog's
context. Per StarRocks docs, SET CATALOG must precede GRANT statements
that target external catalog tables. All create statements run on the
same connection, so the SET CATALOG persists for the subsequent grant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(db): Change OSCONJ column type on form_eye_antseg table (#11502)

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Your PR title must follow Conventional Commits: type(scope): description
Examples: feat(api): add patient search, fix(calendar): correct date
parsing
See CONTRIBUTING.md for details

Please provide context about the nature of your change so that reviewers
understand the motivation and we can generate changelogs.

If this resolves an existing issue, link to one or more issues in the
short description section, e.g. `Fixes #12345`.

-->

#### Short description of what this changes or resolves:
OSCONJ column type on form_ant_antseg table = varchar(25) while column
type = text for ODCONJ and other related columns on table.
This was a problem because users would enter >25 characters in the
OSCONJ field on Anterior Segment section (form_eye_antseg) of the Eye
Form, but only the first 25 characters would save. Related fields in the
section (ODCONJ) allowed and saved entry of >25 characters.

#### Changes proposed in this pull request:
Change OSCONJ column type to text so that it behaves same as related
fields in eye form section.

#### Test Plan
Ran sql upgrade from 8.1.0 after making changes in
8_1_0-to-8_1_1_upgrade.sql without incident and form/field behaved as
expected.

#### Was an AI assistant used? Yes / No
No

<!--
If yes, add an `Assisted-by` trailer to each commit that used AI
assistance:

```
git commit --trailer "Assisted-by: Claude Code" -m "fix(calendar): correct date parsing"
```

Use the name of the specific tool (e.g. `GitHub Copilot`, `Claude Code`,
`ChatGPT`).
If the AI made the commit(s), this trailer was probably added
automatically.
-->

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add proposal for local site modules and plugin architecture

npm package consumption. Site modules become user-owned plugins loaded
from a local directory, keeping the core immutable and the refinement
loop frictionless.

commit.message:
Fix `usethis tool` failing in directories with leading dots (#1903)

* Initial plan

* Fix uv/poetry init failing in directories with leading dots in their name

Pass --name with a sanitized project name to uv init, and fix
get_project_name_from_dir() to strip leading/trailing non-alphanumeric
characters (dots, dashes, underscores) per PEP packaging spec.

Also update poetry init to use get_project_name_from_dir() instead of
the raw directory name for consistency.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/usethis-python/usethis-python/sessions/e415c235-da21-4847-b92c-704513c59e6e

Co-authored-by: nathanjmcdougall <18602289+nathanjmcdougall@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Windows test failure: use PurePosixPath instead of real directories

The test_only_dots test created a directory named "..." which on Windows
is interpreted as parent directory traversal (../..), causing
FileExistsError. Since get_project_name_from_dir() only reads Path.name
and never accesses the filesystem, all tests now use PurePosixPath
synthetic paths instead of creating real directories.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/usethis-python/usethis-python/sessions/b47fb40c-7e7c-44f9-89e3-258b10a5fe34

Co-authored-by: nathanjmcdougall <18602289+nathanjmcdougall@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix basedpyright: replace PurePosixPath with Path/str in tests

PurePosixPath is not assignable to Path | str | None. Use tmp_path /
"name" (Path without mkdir) for most tests, and string paths for
Windows-problematic names ("..." and "project.") since usethis_config
.set() accepts str and converts to Path internally.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/usethis-python/usethis-python/sessions/06111449-4dd0-43b5-87cf-f13293af9a57

Co-authored-by: nathanjmcdougall <18602289+nathanjmcdougall@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix SQLite reserved-word error for 'transaction' table in AI tool executor

The AI generates bare 'FROM transaction' / 'JOIN transaction' without
backticks. 'transaction' is a reserved keyword in SQLite causing:
  near "transaction": syntax error (code 1 SQLITE_ERROR)

Fix: preprocess SQL in both AiToolExecutor and handleNlGenerateStatement
with a regex that quotes every table-reference occurrence of 'transaction'
as `transaction` before execution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(api): add CORS proxy functions for ARM, GitHub, and pricing APIs

B-16 — Three SWA Functions to proxy cross-origin API calls:

- arm-proxy: Forwards requests to management.azure.com with
  Authorization pass-through and default api-version injection
- github-proxy: Forwards requests to api.github.com with
  Accept: application/vnd.github+json and optional auth
- pricing-proxy: Forwards GET requests to prices.azure.com
  retail prices API (public, no auth), with 5-min cache hint

All three:
- Use Azure Functions v4 programming model with wildcard routes
- Propagate upstream HTTP status codes (no error swallowing)
- Pass through relevant rate-limiting response headers
- Use native fetch (Node 18+), TypeScript/ESM

feat: add 10 new features to MailMerge-Pro

1. Email Templates Library - Save/load/delete templates with 3 built-in
   templates (Welcome, Product Update, Invoice) in collapsible Step 3 panel
2. Scheduled Sending - Date/time picker with countdown timer, cancel support,
   interrupted schedule detection via localStorage
3. Email Tracking - Checkbox that enables Graph API read receipts with
   upgrade note for advanced tracking
4. A/B Testing - Toggle enables dual subject/body editors with tabbed UI,
   configurable split ratios (50/50, 70/30, 80/20), deterministic splitting
5. Contact Groups/Segments - Save/load/delete/merge recipient lists in
   localStorage with 3MB size warning
6. HTML Template Import - File picker and drag-and-drop for .html files,
   auto-detects merge field placeholders
7. Signature Auto-Insert - Fetch from Outlook via Graph API or paste
   manually, auto-append toggle persisted in localStorage
8. Rate Limit Dashboard - Daily sent counter with color-coded progress bar,
   auto-suggested delay based on recipient count
9. Multi-Language Support (i18n) - 6 languages (EN/ES/FR/DE/PT/JA) with
   data-i18n attributes and t() translation function
10. Admin Dashboard - Campaign analytics with stats grid, monthly CSS bar
    chart, top recipients, and recent campaigns list

commit.message:
feat: cellular swarm architecture with coherence-gated shards, pipeline, and hardware-aware scaling (#105)

* feat: add cellular swarm architecture with coherence-gated shards, pipeline, and CLI

- Create swarm/memory.py: thread-safe externalised state store
- Create swarm/cell.py: CellShard with deterministic routing, stateless workers, local coherence gate
- Create swarm/pipeline.py: PipelineStage + Pipeline with batch processing and backpressure
- Create swarm/coherence_governor.py: coherence/compute ratio monitoring with consolidation
- Create swarm/orchestrator.py: hardware-aware pool sizing, graceful degradation, shard failover
- Update cli.py: swarm subcommand with --status, --scale auto, --run, --shards, --efficiency-mode
- Update __init__.py: export all swarm symbols
- Add tests/test_swarm_efficiency.py: 94 tests covering routing, lifecycle, backpressure, gating, CLI
